en.extract:
The yellow-throated cuckoo (Chrysococcyx flavigularis) is a species of cuckoo in the family Cuculidae.
It is sparsely present across the African tropical rainforest and Jos Plateau (northern Nigeria). It is threatened by deforestation. 
Yellow-throated cuckoos are obligate brood parasites; they always lay their eggs in other birds' nests. Their only known host is the grey-throated tit-flycatcher, (Myioparus griseigularis).

The '''yellow-throated cuckoo''' ('''''Chrysococcyx flavigularis''''') is a species of [[cuckoo]] in the family [[Cuculidae]].
It is sparsely present across the [[Guineo-Congolian region|African tropical rainforest]] and [[Jos Plateau]] (northern Nigeria). It is threatened by [[deforestation]].<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Mustafa |first1=Ghulam |last2=Usman |first2=Muhammad |last3=Yu |first3=Lisu |last4=afzal |first4=Muhammad Tanvir |last5=Sulaiman |first5=Muhammad |last6=Shahid |first6=Abdul |date=2021-11-09 |title=Multi-label classification of research articles using Word2Vec and identification of similarity threshold |journal=Scientific Reports |volume=11 |issue=1 |page=21900 |doi=10.1038/s41598-021-01460-7 |issn=2045-2322|doi-access=free |pmid=34754057 |pmc=8578475 }}</ref> 

Yellow-throated cuckoos are obligate [[Brood parasitism|brood parasites]]; they always lay their eggs in other birds' nests. Their only known host is the grey-throated tit-flycatcher, (''[[Myioparus griseigularis]]'').<ref>{{Cite book |last1=Erritzoe |first1=Johannes |last2=Mann |first2=Clive |last3=Fuller |first3=Richard |date=2012 |title=Cuckoos of the World |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=avxBNNeRhCYC&dq=Erritz%C3%B8e+et+al.+2012+brood+parasitism&pg=PP1 |access-date=2024-02-27 |publisher=A&C Black |isbn=978-1-4081-4267-7 |language=en}}</ref>
